div.h1: File I/O Performance Analysis: pread vs io_uring
div.p: Environment: Linux Kernel 6.2 | NVMe Gen4 SSD (PCIe 4.0 x4) Workload: 1,000 Random Reads | 4KB Block Size | Single Thread Architecture: x86_64 User-Kernel Ring Buffer Synchronization
The io_uring implementation achieves a 3.3x throughput gain over pread by addressing three primary overheads:

ol.li: System Call Amortization: Reducing user-kernel boundary crossings from $O(N)$ to $O(N/batch_size)$.
Memory Management: "Registered Buffers" provide the kernel with pre-validated, long-lived pointers, eliminating per-I/O virt_to_page lookups and page pinning.
Synchronization: The Submission (SQ) and Completion (CQ) rings are shared memory structures using memory barriers instead of expensive mutexes or spinlocks for coordination.
